title: DisclosureReceipt
description: A recipient's ed25519-signed record that it unsealed a specific sealed event (chp-v0.2.md section 16.1, proposal 0030). The signature covers the canonical header {kind, who, content_hash, payload_commitment, unsealed_at, canonicalization}; the recipient block is the self-attested identity that signs. A non-repudiable disclosure trail over a confidential payload WITHOUT revealing the plaintext. Verification checks structure, the signature against the recipient's key, and who==signing key_id; a caller cross-checks content_hash / payload_commitment against the bundle.

who.description: The recipient key_id that unsealed the event (equals the signing key_id — the receipt names its own signer).

content_hash.description: The chain content_hash of the sealed event that was disclosed.

payload_commitment.description: The committed hash of the disclosed plaintext (sha256(canon(plaintext)) under chp-event-hash-v2) — proves WHICH payload without revealing it.

unsealed_at.description: ISO-8601 time the recipient unsealed the event.

canonicalization.description: The canonicalization scheme for the signed header (e.g. chp-stable-v1).

recipient.description: The recipient's self-attested identity — the key that signs the receipt.
